flow.name: customers-upsert-flow
flow.doc:description: POST /customers — parallel Snowflake upsert per record, email alert on failures

http:listener.doc:name: POST /customers
http:listener.doc:description: Accepts HTTP POST with a JSON array of customer records for Snowflake upsert

http:error-response.http:body: #[output application/json --- {status: "Error", message: "An internal error occurred. Contact support with correlationId: " ++ (vars.correlationId default correlationId)}]

set-variable.doc:name: Set Correlation ID
set-variable.doc:description: Captures Mule correlation ID for structured logging

logger.message: #[%dw 2.0 output application/json --- '[p-customers-api] Request received | correlationId=' ++ vars.correlationId ++ ' | recordCount=' ++ sizeOf(payload)]
logger.doc:name: Log Request Received
logger.doc:description: Logs inbound request with correlationId and record count

ee:transform.doc:name: Validate Request — Array Type and Batch Size
ee:transform.doc:description: Validates JSON array and max batch size limit (default 100)

parallel-foreach.doc:name: Process Customers in Parallel
parallel-foreach.doc:description: Processes each customer concurrently against Snowflake CUSTOMERS_DB.PUBLIC.CUSTOMERS

try.doc:name: Try: Upsert Single Customer
try.doc:description: Upserts one customer in Snowflake; captures errors as Failure results

set-variable.doc:name: Store Current Customer
set-variable.doc:description: Preserves current customer record before Snowflake operations

ee:transform.doc:name: Validate customerEmail Format
ee:transform.doc:description: Validates RFC-5321 email format; throws on null/empty/invalid/too-long

snowflake:select.doc:name: Check Customer Exists by Email
snowflake:select.doc:description: Queries CUSTOMERS.CUSTOMER_EMAIL — returns empty array if new, 1-row array if exists
snowflake:select.snowflake:sql: SELECT CUSTOMER_EMAIL FROM CUSTOMER WHERE CUSTOMER_EMAIL = :customerEmail LIMIT 1

choice.doc:name: Customer Exists in Snowflake?
choice.doc:description: Routes to UPDATE (array size > 0) or INSERT (empty array)

when.doc:name: Customer Exists — UPDATE
when.doc:description: Updates all fields for matching CUSTOMER_EMAIL

snowflake:update.doc:name: Update Customer in Snowflake
snowflake:update.doc:description: Updates FIRST_NAME, LAST_NAME, CONTACT_NUMBER, ADDRESS, LOCATION where CUSTOMER_EMAIL matches
snowflake:update.snowflake:sql: UPDATE CUSTOMER SET FIRST_NAME = :firstName, LAST_NAME = :lastName, CONTACT_NUMBER = :contactNumber, ADDRESS = :address, LOCATION = :location WHERE CUSTOMER_EMAIL = :customerEmail
snowflake:update.snowflake:input-parameters: #[{customerEmail: vars.currentCustomer.customerEmail, firstName: vars.currentCustomer.firstName, lastName: vars.currentCustomer.lastName, contactNumber: vars.currentCustomer.contactNumber default "", address: vars.currentCustomer.address default "", location: vars.currentCustomer.location default ""}]

otherwise.doc:name: Customer New — INSERT
otherwise.doc:description: Inserts new row into CUSTOMERS for new customerEmail

snowflake:insert.doc:name: Insert New Customer into Snowflake
snowflake:insert.doc:description: Inserts CUSTOMER_EMAIL, FIRST_NAME, LAST_NAME, CONTACT_NUMBER, ADDRESS, LOCATION
snowflake:insert.snowflake:sql: INSERT INTO CUSTOMER (CUSTOMER_EMAIL, FIRST_NAME, LAST_NAME, CONTACT_NUMBER, ADDRESS, LOCATION) VALUES (:customerEmail, :firstName, :lastName, :contactNumber, :address, :location)
snowflake:insert.snowflake:input-parameters: #[{customerEmail: vars.currentCustomer.customerEmail, firstName: vars.currentCustomer.firstName, lastName: vars.currentCustomer.lastName, contactNumber: vars.currentCustomer.contactNumber default "", address: vars.currentCustomer.address default "", location: vars.currentCustomer.location default ""}]

on-error-continue.doc:name: On Error Continue — Capture Failure
on-error-continue.doc:description: Captures Snowflake/processing errors as Failure result; other parallel records continue

logger.message: #[%dw 2.0 output application/json --- '[p-customers-api] Record failed | correlationId=' ++ vars.correlationId ++ ' | errorType=' ++ error.description]
logger.doc:name: Log Record Failure
logger.doc:description: Logs failure with errorType only — email omitted for GDPR compliance

ee:transform.doc:name: Build Failure Result
ee:transform.doc:description: Returns customerEmail + status=Failure + error description as message

ee:message.ee:set-payload: %dw 2.0 output application/json --- {customerEmail: vars.currentCustomer.customerEmail default "unknown", status: "Failure", message: error.description default "An unexpected error occurred"}

ee:transform.doc:name: Consolidate Results and Extract Failures
ee:transform.doc:description: Builds final response array; extracts failures; sets httpStatus 207 if any failures

ee:variables.ee:set-variable: %dw 2.0 output application/java --- if (sizeOf(payload filter (item) -> item.status == "Failure") > 0) 207 else 200

logger.message: #[%dw 2.0 output application/json --- '[p-customers-api] Processing complete | correlationId=' ++ vars.correlationId ++ ' | total=' ++ sizeOf(payload) ++ ' | failures=' ++ sizeOf(vars.failures)]
logger.doc:name: Log Processing Summary
logger.doc:description: Logs total vs failures count with correlationId for monitoring

choice.doc:name: Any Failures?
choice.doc:description: Sends SMTPS email alert if any failures; skips otherwise

when.doc:name: Has Failures — Send Email Alert
when.doc:description: Dispatches failure notification with failure-records.json attachment

logger.message: #[%dw 2.0 output application/json --- '[p-customers-api] Failures detected, sending alert | correlationId=' ++ vars.correlationId ++ ' | failureCount=' ++ sizeOf(vars.failures)]
logger.doc:name: Log Failures Alert

set-variable.doc:name: Sanitize correlationId
set-variable.doc:description: Strips non-alphanumeric chars from correlationId to prevent email header injection

otherwise.doc:name: All Success — No Alert
otherwise.doc:description: Skips email when all records succeeded

logger.message: #[%dw 2.0 output application/json --- '[p-customers-api] All records processed successfully | correlationId=' ++ vars.correlationId]
logger.doc:name: Log All Success
logger.doc:description: Confirms successful batch with no failures

error-handler.doc:name: Global Error Handler Ref
error-handler.doc:description: Delegates unhandled flow errors to the global error handler
